news.summary: The article discusses the rise of bot traffic on the internet, with the Bot traffic share hitting its highest level in 2024, up 2% from the previous year according to the 2024 Imperva Bad Bot Report. The article highlights the negative impacts of bots on online interactions, such as artificially inflating engagement metrics and orchestrating scams. It mentions the limitations of current centralized moderation strategies used by social media platforms to combat bots, as they can lead to unfair restrictions on genuine users. The author, Leroy Hofer, advocates for decentralized solutions using blockchain-based decentralized identity (DID) and reputation systems to verify real users without compromising privacy. Decentralized solutions, according to Hofer, empower users to control their digital reputations and reduce the influence of bots. The article concludes by emphasizing the need to move towards decentralized solutions to protect users and restore authenticity to social media, stating that the current centralized measures are ineffective in addressing the bot problem.
news.headline: Decentralization could combat the rise of bots on social media. Bot traffic increased 2% in 2024, reaching its highest level. Centralized moderation strategies are failing. Leroy Hofer suggests a decentralized solution using blockchain to verify real users without compromising privacy. It is time to address the bot problem to protect user trust on social media.

news.summary: StoneX Group Inc. saw a rise in premarket trading on Monday following their announcement of acquiring R.J. O'Brien for approximately $900 million. The deal comprises $625 million in cash and about 3.5 million shares of StoneX common stock. To fund the cash portion, StoneX secured committed bridge financing and plans to issue about $625 million in long-term debt. Additionally, StoneX will be taking on up to $143 million of R.J. O'Brien's debt. R.J. O'Brien is known for its network of introducing brokers, catering to commercial clients. This information was reported by Benzinga.com.
news.headline: StoneX buys R.J. O'Brien for $900 million, gains 300 IBs, adds $6B client float. Deal includes $625M cash and 3.5M shares. StoneX to issue $625M in long-term debt. StoneX also assumes up to $143M in RJO's debt.

The updated design incorporates new, bold hues while still maintaining the signature cap color to preserve the classic Dan-O’s Seasoning appeal. news.summary: Nokia Corporation recently announced that it has secured a contract with Australian cloud provider ResetData. Under this agreement, Nokia will be responsible for providing the networking infrastructure for ResetData's upcoming "AI Factory" data centers in Australia. The project aims to establish sovereign data centers with high-speed, scalable, and reliable networking capabilities to ensure low-latency performance. ResetData's AI Factories will feature innovative liquid immersion cooling technology, making them up to 10 times more efficient than traditional data centers. This advancement is expected to result in a significant reduction of up to 40% in cloud costs and a 45% decrease in emissions. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Nokia wins contract for Australian cloud provider ResetData's AI Factory rollout, promising 40% cost reduction.

news.summary: Viking Therapeutics, Inc. (VKTX), Eli Lilly And Co (LLY), and Novo Nordisk A/S (NVO) stocks have seen an increase in trading on Monday. Pfizer Inc. (PFE) has announced the discontinuation of the development of danuglipron (PF-06882961), an oral glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist for chronic weight management due to potential drug-induced liver injury in a participant. This incident led to Pfizer falling behind competitors like Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk, who are leading the market with weekly injections. Eli Lilly is advancing orforglipron, a small-molecule weight-loss pill set to launch in 2026, providing them with a two-year competitive advantage. Pfizer's move highlights the competitive landscape in weight-loss drug development.
news.headline: Viking Therapeutics, Eli Lilly, and Novo Nordisk stocks surged as Pfizer Inc. halted development of danuglipron due to liver injury risk. Eli Lilly aiming to launch weight-loss pill orforglipron in 2026, gaining a 2-year edge over competitors. Pfizer lags behind in injectable weight loss drug market.

news.headline: Dream Smiles Pediatric Dentistry in Gaithersburg adds 2 new dentists, Dr. Rottenberg & Dr. Morgan, enhancing the team.

news.summary: In San Francisco, Genians and Ramen Inc. have entered a strategic partnership to launch an AI-driven converged network and security platform. Genians is known for its expertise in Universal Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA), while Ramen Inc. specializes in AI-powered Network-as-a-Service (NaaS). This collaboration aims to offer a reliable and secure connectivity solution that caters to both traditional (carpeted) enterprise areas and industrial (uncarpeted) environments. The partnership addresses the growing challenges in the realm of information security and network connectivity.
news.headline: Genians and Ramen team up to deliver AI-powered network and security platform for Smart Manufacturing.

The unemployment rate has seen a decrease to 4%, the lowest it has been in the past two years. This positive trend in job growth has been attributed to the increase in consumer spending and business investments. Additionally, the stock market experienced a significant boost, with the Dow Jones reaching an all-time high of 35,000 points. Experts are hopeful that this upward trajectory will continue in the coming months, leading to further improvements in the overall economic outlook.
news.headline: Nvidia plans to manufacture $500B AI equipment in the US.

news.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange Bybit has refuted claims of charging a $1.4 million listing fee for tokens on its platform, after allegations from a social media user on April 14. Bybit CEO Ben Zhou denied the accusations and requested evidence. Bybit clarified its listing requirements, stating projects need a promotion budget, security deposit, and pass an evaluation process. The exchange asks for a $200,000-$300,000 stablecoin deposit for promotional purposes. Bybit's listing process includes form submissions, internal voting, research, and a review meeting focusing on onchain data, use cases, team credentials, and more. The exchange did not directly address specific claims about providing trial contracts to students in its Campus Ambassador program or using key opinion leaders to silence complaints. No evidence has been provided yet to support these allegations.
news.headline: Bybit refutes $1.4M listing fee allegation, denies school promo accusations involving X. CEO asks for proof, calls for evidence of wrongdoing. Exchange requires $200-300k in stablecoins as deposit for listings, with penalties for unmet targets. Exchange clarifies its evaluation process, emphasizing fundamentals and risk controls. Bybit does not directly address specific claims regarding its Campus Ambassador program. Magazine notes memecoin degeneracy funds anti-aging research.

news.summary: On April 14, 2025, in Pittsburgh, a product designer aimed to develop a new item to maintain low temperatures of water and various fluids in a hydration bladder for an extended duration. news.summary: Solana's SOL has surged over 20% against Ether (ETH) in the last week, with a trader anticipating a potential breakout to $300, reaching new all-time highs. The SOL/ETH ratio hit a record high of 0.080 on April 13, signaling an uptrend, as Solana gained 35% in the past week compared to ETH's 13% increase. The previous peak for the SOL/ETH ratio was 0.093 in January. Analysts predict SOL could hit $300, drawing parallels to ETH's 2021 rally. However, declining network fees and DEX volumes pose challenges for SOL's journey to new highs. Currently priced at $133, SOL is 54.5% below its all-time high, encouraging caution with investments and advising independent research.
news.headline: Solana surges 20% against Ethereum, eyes $300 SOL price. SOL/ETH ratio hits record 0.080. Can SOL reach $300?

news.summary: Circle's Euro Coin (EURC), a euro-pegged stablecoin, is experiencing rapid growth in market cap due to the weakening US dollar caused by the ongoing trade war. The Euro has risen by 2.2% to $1.13, with EURC's market cap increasing from under $84 million at the end of 2024 to over $198 million in mid-April, marking a 136% year-to-date increase. Decentralized finance protocol Aave saw €2.3 million of EURC inflows in April. Circle is benefitting from MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets) regulation compliance, positioning itself favorably in the stablecoin market. The euro's surge against the dollar is expected to continue, with EURC projected to reach a market cap of 400 million euros by year-end through integration with payment systems and multiple blockchains.
news.headline: EURC market cap rises to $198M amidst trade war weakening USD. Euro gains 2.2%, hits $1.13. Aave sees €2.3M inflows in April. Analyst predicts EURC to hit €400M by year-end. Circle benefits from MiCA regulation as EUR pegged stablecoin gains traction. USDC trailing behind.
China and Vietnam signed 45 bilateral cooperation documents - Xinhua.

news.summary: Used car sales are experiencing a significant increase nationwide, with vehicles selling at twice the pace compared to this time last year. This trend is reflected in the latest statistics reporting that in Centreville, Va., as of April 14, 2025, there has been a surge in purchases due to economic uncertainty. Many buyers are motivated to make large acquisitions on Tax Day, highlighting the boost in sales volume and consequently, a rise in vehicle prices.
news.headline: Used car sales surge despite high prices, nationwide cars sell twice as fast as a year ago, driven by economic uncertainty.
